title: • Reflection: What "songbird" in me feels caged or silenced? Wh
content:
• Reflection: What "songbird" in me feels caged or silenced? What can I do to set it free?
    • Day 16
    MORNING
    • Morning Reading: "There was nothing left for him but to efface himself, to destroy the unsuccessful structure of his life, to throw it away, mocked by the gods."
    • Today's Intention: I will practice self-compassion today, especially if I feel I have fallen short of a goal.
    NIGHT
    • Night Reading: "He was full of weariness, full of misery, full of death; there was nothing left in the world that could attract him, that could give him pleasure, that could console him."
    • Reflection: When did I feel "at the end of my rope" today? What small thing helped me find the strength to hold on?
    Day 17
    MORNING
    • Morning Reading: "Then, from a remote part of his soul... he heard a sound. It was one word, one syllable... the ancient beginning and end of all Brahmin prayers, the holy ‘Om,’ which means ‘the Perfect One’ or ‘Perfection.’"
    • Today's Intention: I will find a moment of quiet today to simply listen—not to music or words, but to the sound of life itself.
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 19 (chunk 19/29)
